directive.title: Dashboard performance budget assertion and instrumentation

auditor.summary: The build implements the core performance instrumentation requirements correctly: Server-Timing headers, health/perf endpoint, client-side navigation timing, and fire-and-forget audit logging. However, there are critical issues: (1) lib/prisma.ts imports '@prisma/client' which is not in package.json - the project uses Drizzle ORM, not Prisma; (2) The existing app/api/rfq/route.ts is completely replaced rather than merged, potentially removing existing auth/error handling; (3) The audit_logs schema mapping is correct (entity_type='PERF_TELEMETRY') but acceptance criteria expect a 'category' column that doesn't exist in the actual schema. The build is recoverable with fixes but cannot pass in its current state due to the Prisma dependency issue.

top_issues.description: Imports '@prisma/client' which is NOT listed in package.json dependencies. The project uses Drizzle ORM with 'drizzle-orm' and 'pg' packages, not Prisma. This import will fail at compile time.

The audit_logs schema has columns: id, entity_type, entity_id, action, payload, created_at. There is NO separate 'category' column. The code uses entity_type='PERF_TELEMETRY' which is correct for the schema, but the acceptance criteria expect a 'category' field. The category information is embedded in payload JSON, not as a top-level column. This means criteria 11 and 12 cannot be verified by querying a 'category' column.

This file already exists in the repo at app/api/rfq/route.ts. The generated code completely replaces it with a new implementation that removes any existing auth guards, request parsing, or response shape. The build spec says to 'merge carefully' but the generated code is a full replacement.
